content:
===center ![Elenda, the Dusk Rose](https://media.wizards.com/2017/images/daily/c4rd4r7_lhHgKPFBYp.jpg "Elenda, the Dusk Rose") ===endcenter

Elenda, The First Vampire: Vampire Tribal EDH

[[card:Elenda, the Dusk Rose]], leads a [[symbol:W]][[symbol:B]] vampire tribal commander/EDH deck, focusing on life-drain & death-trigger synergies. The build revolves around getting +1/+1 counters on Elenda, then amassing a board of tokens to utilize, and close out with mass life-draining spells. Tribal synergies and flavor are favored over more efficient cards, in order to form a more cohesively themed deck. [[card:Sorin Markov]] is effectively second-in-command, with him often depicted in the art or flavor-text of non-creature spells.

[[card:Sorin, Vengeful Bloodlord]] & [[card:Anguished Unmaking]] are stand-ins for Oathbreaker.

===accordion ===panel: Introduction
===accordion ===panel: Commander - "The First and the Faithful."
Elenda, the Dusk Rose is a commander which can have various synergies to build around. She can be a tribal commander for both vampires and knights. She can be utilized for aristocrats style builds, because she has triggered abilities when another creature dies and when she dies. She can be utilized for +1/+1 counter builds because of her first triggered ability, or for token builds because of her second triggered ability. She can be utilized for life gain builds because both she and the tokens she creates have lifelink. And while you can focus on one particular strategy, this deck tries to synthesize these strategies around her, while maintaining flavor with a heavy vampire tribal theme. The other reasons to choose her include:

  • Color Identity - Elenda's [[symbol:W]][[symbol:B]] colors give you access to all of the advantages in these colors, including targeted permanent removal ( such as [[card:Anguished Unmaking]] and [[card:Vindicate]]), the best and most efficient board wipes, the best tutors, as well as access to sufficient reanimation / recursion. The strengths in one color help to supplement the weaknesses in the other. A true yin-yang combination.

  • Mythology - Also known as The First Vampire, The Bat of the East, and Saint Elenda, she is the progenitor of the vampires of the Legion of Dusk on Ixalan. Initially from the Church of Dusk, tasked with retrieving and protecting [[card:The Immortal Sun]] after it was stolen, she placed herself under an enchanted sleep at the [[card:Tomb of the Dusk Rose]], while awaiting the awakening of the Golden City of Orazca. In her absence, her legend only grew throughout the realms of Ixalan until she became a pivotal part of its history.

  • Aesthetics - The art on [[card:Elenda, the Dusk Rose]] by Chris Rahn is amazing. The golden color scheme of the background art is especially vibrant, really stands out in foil, and supplements the gold frame. The fact that your commander card looks like a treasure in and of itself, is evocative of the Legion of Dusk conquistador and treasure token sub-themes in the deck.

  • Underestimated - A [[symbol:4]] CMC commander that comes out as a 1/1 is not threatening on the surface. This allows you to play under-the-radar in the early game, until you're ready for an unexpected blowout. She's also a far less popular commander, therefore your deck strategy is much less likely to be known.

===endpanel ===panel: Color Identity - "Hair like a raven's wings and nails like lightning's edge!" [[symbol:W]][[symbol:B]] are the colors often associated with the _Orzhov Syndicate_ of Ravnica, which embodies the balance between good and evil, existing in the zone of the morally gray, seeking power and luxury, building hierarchy and structure in order to enact order upon the world, but [playing by their own rules](https://magic.wizards.com/en/articles/archive/making-magic/playing-their-own-rules-2006-03-27-0 "Playing By Their Own Rules"). While the two colors are diametric opposites, together they form a powerful combination, complementing each other's weaknesses. There are many reasons to run this color combination, including the following:

  • Permanent Removal - Black excels at creature removal, while white excels at artifact or enchantment removal. Together the are potent enough to eliminate any threat that might block your path to victory, removing them from the game entirely.
    Examples: [[card:Anguished Unmaking]], [[card:Vindicate]], [[card:Utter End]], [[card:Despark]]

  • Tutelage - The most powerful and most efficient tutor spells exist within these colors, enabling you to find the cards you need when you need them, and allowing for deck flexibility and consistency when required.
    Examples: [[card:Vampiric Tutor]], [[card:Demonic Tutor]], [[card:Enlightened Tutor]]

  • Board Wipes - The most powerful and most efficient board wipes also exist within these colors. While we don't run them due to the complexities of our commander, there are a wide range of options available to our disposal should we need them.
    Examples: [[card:Damnation]], [[card:Wrath of God]], [[card:Toxic Deluge]]

  • Life Drain - A powerful effect exclusive to these colors, that represent either vampiricism or life being paid as a tithe to the church. Utilizing these effects can give us massive life swings to our advantage, creating a healthy buffer of life, that can also be utilized to our advantage.
    Examples: [[card:Exsanguinate]], [[card:Debt to the Deathless]]

  • Pay Life - Black knows that life is a resource, which can be used as payment to us, but also in turn used to pay for power and knowledge. White emphasizes the continuity of life, and helps restore it through lifelink or life gain. Together they work synergistically by amassing us life to utilize for gain.
    Examples: [[card:Necropotence]], [[card:Greed]]

  • Sacrifice - Both colors know the benefits of sacrifice, either for themselves or for the greater good. Nothing is forbidden from being utilized as a resource, including our own creatures. This gives us a variety of ways to protect our own board, while getting incremental gains.
    Examples: [[card:Pitiless Pontiff]], [[card:Ashnod's Altar]]

  • Resurrection - Both colors acknowledge the transience of death, that it is not the end of the journey, merely a stop along the way. Creatures are frequently brought back from death, either via immortality or necromancy. This gives our creatures resilience and reusability, utilizing our graveyard as an additional resource.
    Examples: [[card:Gift of Immortality]], [[card:Bloodline Necromancer]]

  • Wealth - In combination, these colors often represent taxes, luxury, and the accumulation of wealth. This is embodied in our deck through the use of [[card:Treasure]] tokens.
    Examples: [[card:Smothering Tithe]], [[card:Revel in Riches]]

  • Tribal - The most frequent tribes seen in these colors include vampires, knights, clerics and spirits. [[card:Elenda, the Dusk Rose]] is a vampire knight who is a part of the Church of Dusk, embodying many of these tribes simultaneously, making her a worthy tribal leader. Each tribe has unique benefits of their own, but they all commonly provide multiple tokens for us to utilize.
    Examples: [[card:Etchings of the Chosen]]

  • Patience - These colors like to play the long game, gradually taxing or draining our opponents, gaining incremental advantages through a war of attrition, until the right moment to strike. We are weaker in the early game without the explosive land ramp of green, or the impulsive action of red.
    Examples: [[card:Blind Obedience]], [[card:Attrition]]

===endpanel ===panel: Alternate Options - "Others of our order." As previously mentioned above, **[[card:Elenda, the Dusk Rose]]** can be a complex commander to pilot, but she brings a broad range of options and strategies to the table. There are several alternative commanders within [[symbol:W]][[symbol:B]] to consider, I will discuss them below, including how they may vary the gameplay strategy of the deck.

  • [[card:Ayli, Eternal Pilgrim]] - Ayli is an incredibly strong substitute as commander. She boasts a very low mana cost, being a deathtouch combat deterrent, being a sacrifice outlet, having life gain synergies, and having targeted nonland permanent removal. As such, she has a lot of resilience, being able to be re-cast several times per game, and utility with her multiple abilities. If your opponents can make accurate threat assessments, she will draw more hate, but if played politically, she is incredibly powerful. She is however... not a vampire. She would be my top recommendation for a substitute if flavor is not an issue for you.

  • [[card:Teysa Karlov]] - She currently fits as part of the 99, however as commander she favors an aristocrats strategy by doubling your death triggers, and a token strategy by providing them with deathtouch and lifelink keywords. As such, she skews the deck list in favor of that direction, over the life gain synergies in the deck. She has a similar mana cost as Elenda, however she is less likely to be a synergistic piece of a game winning combo, she instead helps you get there faster once she's out. She's also not a vampire, but given her nobility and near-immortal age, she is essentially an honorary one.

  • [[card:Vish Kal, Blood Arbiter]] - He pilots most similarly to Elenda, in that he utilises +1/+1 counter synergies, and will often run a similar deck list. Having a sacrifice outlet available from the command zone when needed can be a huge benefit, however this is balanced by his significantly higher mana cost. The high mana cost ends up being a significant flaw, particularly if he is removed, and needed to be re-cast. As such, he generally plays slower, and generally only participates in the end game as a combo finisher.

  • [[card:Vona, Butcher of Magan]] - Vona pushes the deck more towards a life gain direction. When she hits the battlefield, her ability to destroy nonland permanents makes her a more obvious target and threat, and can potentially draw you hate. As such, despite her potent ability, without haste enablers or untappers, it can be difficult to get many activations without a decent amount of protection. Combined with the limitation of only being able to activate the ability during your turn, she has less flexibility as a political tool, and gives you less time to setup your game plan.

===endpanel ===panel: Psychography - "Those who would use its power for their own selfish ends." The psychographic profile is a term coined by MtG Head Designer **Mark Rosewater**, that separates players into categories which determines _why_ a player may enjoy playing a deck, or playing the game. A _Timmy/Tammy_ plays to experience something, a _Johnny/Jenny_ plays to express something, a _Spike_ plays to prove something. More in-depth information can be found [here](https://magic.wizards.com/en/articles/archive/making-magic/timmy-johnny-and-spike-revisited-2006-12-18). There may be a little something in this deck for everyone, but it is definitely geared more toward Johnny/Jenny players. The following are reasons why you might enjoy playing this deck.

  • Vampires - This deck tries to embody the culture of the vampires of Ixalan, namely the Legion of Dusk. This includes spells which drain the life from our opponents, as well as seeking and hoarding treasure, while destroying anything in our path. If you want a deck that is high on flavor (and I don't mean the taste of blood!), then this is for you.

  • Combos - This deck has plenty of combos and synergies to utilize. And these range between simple but fast two card staple combos, to more complex multi-card synergies. For example, try pulling off: [[card:Elenda, the Dusk Rose]] + [[card:Ashnod's Altar]] + [[card:Animation Module]] + [[card:Cruel Celebrant]]. If you enjoy putting together different pieces of the combo puzzle, then this is for you.

  • Strategic - This deck is helmed by a complicated commander. Her death trigger ability means that you'll have to strategically choose between building up counters, or sacrificing her for vampire tokens to combo off. Oftentimes, knowing when to cast her, knowing when to wipe the board, and knowing when to sacrifice her, is dependent on the board state of your opponents and tactical timing. If you enjoy biding your time, setting yourself up for the major endgame play, then this is for you.

  • Power Level - Utilizing the power level descriptions from The Command Zone, I would describe the goal of this deck list as aiming to achieve a power level sitting between focused and optimized (or a rating of 6-7 out of 10). It sits at a point that maintains an element of theme and flavour, while also being relatively efficient and having multiple synergies / strategies / avenues to winning. It is balanced to be able to have an enjoyable match-up against most decks you'll come across. However, due to the limitations of the commander herself, as well as the flavor restrictions, this deck will never come close to reaching the realms of competitive decks in cEDH.

===endpanel ===endaccordion ===endpanel ===endaccordion ===accordion ===panel: Strategy
===accordion ===panel: Adding Counters - "This dark power that we took into ourselves." With Elenda out, focus should be on getting +1/+1 counters on her to power her up. This can be achieved either by killing our opponents creatures, or sacrificing our own. We can accelerate this with synergies from certain cards.

  • [[card:Animation Module]] - Slow, but can be a mana-sink for additional counters when we need them.

  • [[card:Blade of the Bloodchief]] - When attached to Elenda, each creature death nets us three +1/+1 counters instead of one, accelerating her power accumulation dramatically.

  • [[card:Cathars' Crusade]] - Synergizes with Elenda's death trigger to produce a big bodied board. And can help supplement Elenda with +1/+1 counters as you build your board further. Can be a suitable alternative win condition.

  • [[card:Cordial Vampire]] - Not only will this double the amount of +1/+1 counters on Elenda with each creature death, it will power up the rest of our board if necessary.

  • [[card:Teysa Karlov]] - As the [[card:Panharmonicon]] of death triggers, not only will she double the amount of +1/+1 counters placed on Elenda, when Elenda dies with her on the field, it will double the amount of tokens she produces, essentially netting us four for the price of one!

  • [[card:The Ozolith]] - A safety net that allows us to keep any +1/+1 counters, should Elenda be removed from the board. Also synergises with [[card:Cordial Vampire]] and [[card:Patron of the Vein]], by allowing us to move counters from sacrifice fodder to Elenda.

  • [[card:Indulgent Aristocrat]] - Not included in this list, but a useful substitute, due to the low CMC, and the ability to add +1/+1 counters when required.

  • [[card:Patron of the Vein]] - No longer included in the deck list. Doubles the amount of +1/+1 counters gained on Elenda when an opponent's creature dies, and also serves to hose your opponents' graveyard strategies.

===endpanel ===panel: Sacrifice Outlets - "Her sacrifice is our survival." Sacrifice outlets form the crux of our strategy in a number of important ways. Initially, by sacrificing our own creatures, they will set up death triggers, including getting counters onto Elenda. Once Elenda has built up enough power, we need ways to get her to the graveyard at will in order to produce tokens. Certain outlets will also reap their own benefits, and have their own synergies.

  • [[card:Ashnod's Altar]] / [[card:Phyrexian Altar]] - Can generate mana to fuel our strategies. Allows us to go infinite with Elenda, and either [[card:Animation Module]] or [[card:Nim Deathmantle]].

  • [[card:Etchings of the Chosen]] - Also provides a form of protection to your creatures by being able to provide targeted indestructible.

  • [[card:High Market]] - Useful sacrifice outlet, as lands are much harder to dispose of than creatures. Life gain trigger can also set up life drain loops when needed.

  • [[card:Skullclamp]] - Can be utilized as a sacrifice outlet on the vampire tokens to draw cards when needed. When desperate for [[symbol:3]], you can sacrifice an unpowered Elenda and her subsequent two tokens for six cards, which is high value card draw.

  • [[card:Viscera Seer]] - Costless sacrifice outlet, who can smooth out draws with the scry ability. Low CMC means that he can come down early to set up.

  • [[card:Bartolomé del Presidio]] - Costless sacrifice outlet, and builds up +1/+1 counters on his body.

  • [[card:Yahenni, Undying Partisan]] - Costless sacrifice outlet, who also benefits from death triggers and +1/+1 counter gains. Can function as an indestructible beat-stick and blocker when needed.

  • [[card:Indulgent Aristocrat]] - Not included in this list, but a useful substitute, due to the low CMC, and the +1/+1 counter synergies.

===endpanel ===panel: Death Triggers - "Drinkers of life, devourers of names." Given that Elenda is heavily reliant on death triggers, we will utilize vampires that also have synergies with death triggers to maximize our gains.

  • [[card:Blood Artist]] / [[card:Cruel Celebrant]] - The incremental life drain over the course of the game will set your opponents up to be finished off by a massive swing. Suitable substitutes include [[card:Falkenrath Noble]] or [[card:Vindictive Vampire]] (excluded due to high CMC), and [[card:Zulaport Cutthroat]] (excluded due to tribal requirements).

  • [[card:Revel in Riches]] - For each creature that dies over the course of the game, we will slowly build our mana pool. [[card:Revel in Riches]] allows us to utilize the mana more flexibly, and is an alternate win condition.

  • [[card:Blade of the Bloodchief]] / [[card:Cordial Vampire]] / [[card:Yahenni, Undying Partisan]] - Each death powers up a beatstick for you to either block or threaten with.

  • [[card:Kalitas, Traitor of Ghet]] / [[card:Patron of the Vein]] - No longer included on the deck list. Helps to power your board, but also counters graveyard reanimation strategies of your opponents by exiling their creatures. ([[card:Patron of the Vein]] still allows death triggers to resolve, while [[card:Kalitas, Traitor of Ghet]] is a replacement effect.)

  • [[card:Teysa Karlov]] - She is an absolute powerhouse in this deck. By doubling death triggers, she doubles the gains of the above cards.

===endpanel ===panel: Board Wipes - "Empires are temporary." The suite of board wipes that Elenda will like to see running differs from the typical deck. Unfortunately, when Elenda is taken out by a board wipe she will only create a number of tokens equal to her power at that time, as she won't be on the board to gain the +1/+1 counters from her death trigger prior to creating tokens. This creates the unusual problem where a complete board wipe will often be to her detriment. The elegant solution is to run conditional board wipes that keep her on the board, from which you may sacrifice her afterwards. Oftentimes, this does mean you need to think strategically about utilizing targeted removal to pick off the remaining threats.

  • [[card:Divine Reckoning]] - Flashback allows this card to pull double duty. Keeping Elenda on the field will often mean she becomes a huge-bodied threat against whatever remains.

  • [[card:Single Combat]] - By denying players the ability to cast more creatures or planeswalkers, you slow your opponents ability to rebuild their boards. Keep in mind that this also affects you until the end of your *next* turn too, so it's often wise to cast this in the post-combat main phase. If properly setup with a sacrifice outlet, you can subsequently sacrifice Elenda prior to your next turn and go wide while your opponents have minimal blockers.

  • [[card:Tragic Arrogance]] - Even better than the above board wipes, because *you* get to choose what your opponents keep. The asymmetry of this allows you to organize a board state to your advantage, and will often set you up to take out an opponent.

  • [[card:Cataclysmic Gearhulk]] - No longer included in the deck list. [[card:Cataclysm]] on a body. When cast at the right time, even without Elenda on the field, you can often pull out ahead.

  • [[card:Austere Command]] - No longer included in the deck list. However, the flexibility of this board wipe means that you can use this as a complete board wipe when Elenda isn't on the field, get rid of problem artifacts and/or enchantments, or clear out go-wide token strategies while powering up Elenda.

===endpanel ===panel: Resurrection - "The way to immortality." Previously, in order for Elenda's death trigger to take effect, you had to forego the command zone replacement effect. With the Core 2021 rules change, commander death triggers now take effect before a state based action returns them to the command zone (similar to how tokens set off death triggers, but don't remain in the graveyard). However, having her death trigger go off several times in a game, will result in a high amount of commander tax, so having a couple of options to bring her back from the graveyard is helpful.

  • [[card:Bloodline Necromancer]] - Has the added benefit of bringing an extra body, and a vampire for synergy at that.

  • [[card:Bojuka Bog]] - Useful to hose an opponent's graveyard reanimator strategy, but if desperate, you can target your own graveyard, and utilize the command zone replacement effect to pull Elenda back to the command zone rather than exile.

  • [[card:Gift of Immortality]] - So long as Elenda isn't targeted twice within the same turn, this is a reusable way to bring Elenda back from the graveyard.

  • [[card:Nim Deathmantle]] - Another reusable way to bring Elenda back from the graveyard, provided you keep enough mana open.

  • [[card:Sorin, Vengeful Bloodlord]] - Can bring Elenda back on demand if desperate, but if you can keep him around long enough to use his [[symbol:loyalty-plus2]] ability a couple of times, he is reusable as well.

===endpanel ===endaccordion ===endpanel ===endaccordion
===accordion ===panel: Win Conditions
===accordion ===panel: Combos - "Our most holy mission." While the deck has a number of combos with Elenda, it is not wholly reliant on her to finish out games. Often, a timely cast spell will result in a sufficient life swing, which either kills on resolution, or leaves the opponents with minimal life for us to close out.

  • [[card:Elenda, the Dusk Rose]] + [[card:Ashnod's Altar]] / [[card:Phyrexian Altar]] + [[card:Animation Module]] - [[card:Animation Module]] can initiate the combo itself for [[symbol:4]] mana, or whenever a +1/+1 counter is placed on Elenda. With a servo token on the field, it can be sacrificed to [[card:Ashnod's Altar]], spend [[symbol:1]] to create another servo token, netting you a +1/+1 counter on Elenda, and a surplus of [[symbol:1]] mana. Repeated, this generates infinite mana, infinite +1/+1 counters on Elenda, and infinite vampire tokens when Elenda is sacrificed. [[card:Phyrexian Altar]] accomplishes the same thing, but you won't get a surplus of infinite mana until you sacrifice Elenda and the infinite tokens. With [[card:Blood Artist]] or [[card:Cruel Celebrant]] on the field, you can drain the life of your opponents completely.

  • [[card:Elenda, the Dusk Rose]] + [[card:Ashnod's Altar]] / [[card:Phyrexian Altar]] + [[card:Nim Deathmantle]] - By stacking the death trigger of Elenda first, you can utilize the mana from sacrificing her and the vampire token to [[card:Ashnod's Altar]] to pay for the [[card:Nim Deathmantle]] ability to bring back Elenda. Given that [[card:Nim Deathmantle]] gives +2/+2, each subsequent time you sacrifice Elenda, you will generate a surplus of 2 vampire tokens. This again, allows you to generate infinite mana, infinite +1/+1 counters on Elenda, infinite vampire tokens, and infinite death triggers. [[card:Phyrexian Altar]] requires an additional creature to create an infinite loop, and 2 additional creatures to create a surplus of infinite tokens.

  • [[card:Elenda, the Dusk Rose]] + [[card:Phyrexian Altar]] + [[card:Teysa Karlov]] - This combo can be initiated as long as you have sufficient sacrifice fodder to off-set commander tax. Assuming no additional commander tax, this combo works with just an additional creature on the field. Sacrifice the excess creature to [[card:Phyrexian Altar]], due to [[card:Teysa Karlov]]'s trigger, Elenda gets 2 +1/+1 counters. Sacrifice Elenda to [[card:Phyrexian Altar]], due to [[card:Teysa Karlov]]'s trigger, you will get 6 vampire tokens. Sacrifice 4 tokens, and you have sufficient mana to recast Elenda with her commander tax. Repeat this loop for infinite mana, infinite +1/+1 counters on Elenda, infinite vampire tokens, and infinite death triggers (and infinite commander tax).

  • [[card:Elenda, the Dusk Rose]] + [[card:Phyrexian Altar]] + [[card:Cordial Vampire]] + [[card:Anointed Procession]] - This combo replaces [[card:Teysa Karlov]] with two other cards to achieve the same effect. Assuming no additional commander tax, this combo works with just an additional creature on the field. Sacrifice the excess creature to [[card:Phyrexian Altar]], due to the trigger of [[card:Cordial Vampire]], Elenda gets multiple +1/+1 counters. Sacrifice Elenda to [[card:Phyrexian Altar]], due to [[card:Anointed Procession]]'s trigger, you will get double vampire tokens. Sacrifice 4 tokens, and you have sufficient mana to recast Elenda with her commander tax. Repeat this loop for infinite mana, infinite +1/+1 counters on Elenda, infinite vampire tokens, and infinite death triggers (and infinite commander tax).

  • [[card:Elenda, the Dusk Rose]] + [[card:Phyrexian Altar]] + [[card:Felisa, Fang of Silverquill]] - This combo replaces [[card:Teysa Karlov]] with Felisa to achieve the same effect. Assuming no additional commander tax, this combo works with 3 additional creatures on the field. Sacrifice the excess creature to [[card:Phyrexian Altar]], Elenda gets 3 +1/+1 counters. Sacrifice Elenda to [[card:Phyrexian Altar]], due to [[card:Felisa, Fang of Silverquill]]'s trigger, you will get 3 inkling tokens in addition to 4 vampire tokens from Elenda (7 tokens total). Sacrifice 2 tokens, and you have sufficient mana to recast Elenda with her commander tax, and have surplus tokens to spare. Repeat this loop for infinite mana, infinite +1/+1 counters on Elenda, infinite vampire tokens, and infinite death triggers (and infinite commander tax).

  • [[card:Elenda, the Dusk Rose]] + [[card:Phyrexian Altar]] + [[card:Blade of the Bloodchief]] - Similarly, this combo can be initiated, provided that Elenda has the [[card:Blade of the Bloodchief]] equipped, and there is sufficient sacrifice fodder. Assuming no additional commander tax, this combo works with 2 additional creatures on the field. Sacrifice the excess creatures to [[card:Phyrexian Altar]], due to the trigger of [[card:Blade of the Bloodchief]], Elenda gets 6 +1/+1 counters. Sacrifice Elenda to [[card:Phyrexian Altar]], creating 7 vampire tokens. Sacrifice 4 tokens, and you have sufficient mana to recast Elenda with her commander tax, and re-equip [[card:Blade of the Bloodchief]]. Repeat this loop for infinite mana, infinite +1/+1 counters on Elenda, infinite vampire tokens, and infinite death triggers (and infinite commander tax).

  • [[card:Exquisite Blood]] / [[card:Bloodthirsty Conqueror]] + [[card:Sanguine Bond]] / [[card:Vito, Thorn of the Dusk Rose]] / [[card:Marauding Blight-Priest]] / [[card:Vizkopa Guildmage]] - The triggers from [[card:Exquisite Blood]] and [[card:Sanguine Bond]] will loop off each other infinitely, as soon as either you gain life or an opponent loses life from any source, resulting in infinite life for you, and an instant loss for your opponents. [[card:Vito, Thorn of the Dusk Rose]] and [[card:Marauding Blight-Priest]] share redundancy with [[card:Sanguine Bond]], but also benefit from being creatures which can be tutored by [[card:Forerunner of the Legion]].

  • [[card:Blood Tribute]] + [[card:Sanguine Bond]] / [[card:Vito, Thorn of the Dusk Rose]] / [[card:Vizkopa Guildmage]] - As long as you pay the kicker cost for [[card:Blood Tribute]], the life gain trigger will allow you to instantly kill an opponent. With [[card:Vizkopa Guildmage]], you'll end up hurting your other opponents quite hard too.

  • [[card:Debt to the Deathless]] / [[card:Exsanguinate]] + [[card:Sanguine Bond]] / [[card:Vito, Thorn of the Dusk Rose]] / [[card:Vizkopa Guildmage]] - If [[symbol:X]] = [[symbol:10]] for [[card:Exsanguinate]], or [[symbol:X]] = [[symbol:5]] for [[card:Debt to the Deathless]], the synergy will allow you to instantly kill an opponent (provided that you still have 3 opponents). This is also an outlet to dump infinite mana from the earlier combo synergies.

  • [[card:Malakir Bloodwitch]] / [[card:Sanctum Seeker]] + [[card:Sanguine Bond]] / [[card:Vito, Thorn of the Dusk Rose]] / [[card:Vizkopa Guildmage]] - With sufficient vampires on the field (ten vampires required for three opponents at full life), the synergy will similarly allow you to instantly kill an opponent. Suitable substitutes include [[card:Gray Merchant of Asphodel]] (excluded because he does not synergize with Elenda's vampire tokens).

  • [[card:Bloodline Necromancer]] + [[card:Ashnod's Altar]] + [[card:Nim Deathmantle]] - By sacrificing [[Bloodline Necromancer]] and one other creature to [[card:Ashnod's Altar]] to pay for the [[card:Nim Deathmantle]] ability, you can bring back [[Bloodline Necromancer]] and one other creature in your graveyard as an infinite loop. This grants infinite ETB and death triggers which can either combo with Elenda, or life draining finishers including [[Malakir Bloodwitch]] to end the game.

  • [[card:Revel in Riches]] + [[card:Smothering Tithe]] / [[card:Monologue Tax]] - This is an alternative win condition, which happens to synergize well with the deck given that you are aiming to utilize death triggers. If [[card:Smothering Tithe]] or [[card:Monologue Tax]] has generated sufficient treasure tokens, flashing in [[card:Revel in Riches]] with [[card:Emergence Zone]] during your opponent's end step, means your opponents won't have time to stop you.
===endpanel ===panel: Alternative Options - "Do you not feel the change in the city?" There are a number of common combos or synergies in [[symbol:W]][[symbol:B]] aristocrat style decks which can also be considered.

  • [[card:Karmic Guide]] + [[card:Reveillark]] - This is an alternative option for recursive loops of death triggers and sacrifice fodder in our colors. Excluded due to poor synergy and flavor with the rest of the deck.

  • [[card:Reassembling Skeleton]] + [[card:Pitiless Plunderer]] - This is an alternative option for recursive loops of death triggers and sacrifice fodder in our colors. Reassembling Skeleton can be brought back a combination of mana producers including [[card:Pitiless Plunderer]], [[card:Pawn of Ulamog]], or [[card:Phyrexian Altar]].
===endpanel ===endaccordion ===endpanel ===endaccordion ===accordion ===panel: Gameplan
===accordion ===panel: Mulligan (T0) - "She was born mortal, a warrior nun." Under the current [London Mulligan](https://magic.wizards.com/en/articles/archive/news/london-mulligan-2019-06-03 "The London Mulligan") rules, each time you mulligan, you draw seven cards, then put a number of cards equal to the number of times you have mulliganed this game on the bottom of your library in any order. Given the multiplayer format of Commander/EDH, you also get one "_free_" mulligan per game. This allows for greater card selection and consistency in the opening hand, and should therefore be taken full advantage of.

  • Mana Sources - Prioritize having 4 mana sources (either land or 1-2 CMC ramp) in your starting hand, or else you risk becoming mana screwed. Without any land ramp, it is important to be able to consistently hit your early land drops. With 37 lands in the deck, statistically there is over 70% chance to hit your first three land drops, and you should prioritize your mulligans to improving this.

  • Color Sources - Also try to have at least 1 source of each color. Once you have 2 [[symbol:W]] sources, focus on putting down your [[card:Swamp]] over [[card:Plains]], because only one card in the deck needs double [[symbol:W]], whereas there are multiple cards that will lean heavily in [[symbol:B]].

  • Converted Mana Cost - Don't keep expensive CMC cards that you won't be able to play early, because they'll sit there doing nothing. The deck has plenty of ways to either draw out or tutor for the game winning pieces it needs later on. They're safer tucked away in your library, than in your hand where they are vulnerable to discard effects.

===endpanel ===panel: Early (T1-4) - "She gave us life!" The goals of the early game are to ramp, set yourself up, and minimize your chances of drawing hate from your opponents. I've targeted a mana curve sitting between 3-3.5, with almost a third of the cards hitting a CMC between 1-2. This should allow for plenty of early plays if you keep the right hand.

  • Ramp - Almost always a goal for any deck in the early game, try to have an early ramp piece in your hand, because one of your opponents will inevitably have one, and you'll quickly fall behind without. Just be wary that a turn one [[card:Sol Ring]] will draw hate, which we want to avoid. Otherwise [[card: Arcane Signet]], [[card:Fellwar Stone]], [[card:Mind Stone]], [[card:Orzhov Signet]], and [[card:Talisman of Hierarchy]] are all suitable.

  • Setup - You want early pieces to help accelerate Elenda doing what she does best. Cards such as [[card:Animation Module]], [[card:Blade of the Bloodchief]] or [[card:Cordial Vampire]] look relatively harmless on their own, but will help further Elenda's strategy when she drops. Similarly, [[card:Blood Artist]] or [[card:Cruel Celebrant]] will help with incremental life drain moving forward.

  • Sacrifice - An early sacrifice outlet also gives you a safety net before dropping Elenda. If she gets targeted, you can sacrifice her, and either get some early tokens or return her to the command zone. [[card:Viscera Seer]], [[card:Pitiless Pontiff]], [[card:Etchings of the Chosen]], or [[card:Yahenni, Undying Partisan]] all fit in this category. I would advise against dropping [[card:Ashnod's Altar]] / [[card:Phyrexian Altar (UMA)]] early, because it is a combo piece that can draw hate, or be removed before being properly utilised.

  • Deter - You have plenty of low cost removal in the deck (such as [[card:Go for the Throat]], [[card:Anguished Unmaking]], [[card:Mortify]], [[card:Vindicate]], [[card:Gatekeeper of Malakir]]), with which you can use to threaten anyone wanting to point creatures your way. Most of the time though, you'll want to save them for game changing threats. You also have plenty of low cost vampires to occupy your field and make you less of an easy target. Generally, you'll not want to trade creatures because the utility takes priority over damage mitigation, especially because you'll likely be able to gain the life back later.

===endpanel ===panel: Mid (T5-8) - "She searched for centuries and returned." Heading into the mid game, your aim should be finding the right time to drop [[card:Elenda, the Dusk Rose]] for maximum gains. This is usually in situations when you have some early synergy pieces in play to help her gain counters, ways to either protect or resurrect her, or when your opponents have a healthy board that you can take advantage of with a board wipe.

  • Recharge - After dropping all your early plays, you'll need ways to refill your hand. [[card:Sign in Blood]] helps to smooth your draws, while [[card:Syphon Mind]] and [[card:Champion of Dusk]] are more substantial. [[card:Greed]], [[card:Necropotence]], and [[card:Twilight Prophet]] are more sustainable over time.

  • Sustain - Having an insurance line for Elenda being targeted is important, otherwise she'll become a wasted play. Cards such as [[card:Gift of Immortality]], [[card:Nim Deathmantle]] or [[card:Etchings of the Chosen]] will keep her on the field and sustain your gameplan. Similarly, [[card:Bloodline Necromancer]] or [[card:Sorin, Vengeful Bloodlord]] can bring her back on demand if she hits the graveyard.

  • Purge - An optimal scenario which can set you up for the end game, is when your opponents feel that they've got themselves going with a board full of creatures, while you have a board wipe in hand (*such as* [[card:Divine Reckoning]], [[card:Single Combat]], [[card:Tragic Arrogance]] *or* [[card:Cataclysmic Gearhulk]]). This will often pave the way for Elenda to accumulate a large amount of counters, and either swing big, or produce lots of tokens to swing wide. Similarly, having either [[card:Kalitas, Traitor of Ghet]] or [[card:Revel in Riches]] out during a board wipe will often put you at an advantage to set you up for a finisher.

===endpanel ===panel: Late (T9+) - "She was a genius! She was a visionary!" At this point, you should be digging for your win conditions, and have sufficient mana to cast them and win the game on the spot. Even if you don't kill all your opponents at once, usually a massive life drain spell will give you enough of a life total buffer to finish off whoever is left the next turn.

  • Tutor - The deck contains two tutors, of which [[card:Vampiric Tutor]] is more flexible, efficient, and powerful. You generally want to save tutoring until you know which win condition you're aiming for. Of note, the primary targets for [[card:Forerunner of the Legion]] will be [[card:Malakir Bloodwitch]] (*who can be a repeatable drain with* [[card:Nim Deathmantle]]), or [[card:Vito, Thorn of the Dusk Rose]] (*who acts as a redundancy copy of* [[card:Sanguine Bond]]).

  • Combo - The majority of the win condition combo pieces fall into one of two categories, either as a combo enabler, or a combo finisher. Having one of each will help you end the game. The redundancy in each category means that you'll often find a line towards a win at some point in the game.
    • Combo Enablers: [[card:Sanguine Bond]] / [[card:Vito, Thorn of the Dusk Rose]] / [[card:Vizkopa Guildmage]]
    • Combo Finishers: [[card:Exquisite Blood]] / [[card:Bloodthirsty Conqueror]] / [[card:Blood Tribute]] / [[card:Exsanguinate]] / [[card:Debt to the Deathless]] / [[card:Malakir Bloodwitch]] / [[card:Sanctum Seeker]]

===endpanel ===endaccordion ===endpanel ===endaccordion ===accordion ===panel: Metagame
===accordion ===panel: Notable Exclusions - "I see I must teach them what they forgot." Exclusions will often either be due to a lack of synergy either mechanically or flavorfully with the theme of the deck. However, if one wishes to forego flavor, and optimize the deck further, the following cards are for consideration.

  • [[card: Drivnod, Carnage Dominus]] - Effect redundancy for [[card: Teysa Karlov]]. Very strong effect and synergies with the rest of the deck, Cut due to not being on flavour, but should be highly considered due to strong combo potential with Elenda.

  • [[card:Mondrak, Glory Dominus]] / [[card: Ojer Taq, Deepest Foundation]] / [[card: Elspeth, Storm Slayer]] - Effect redundancy for [[card: Anointed Procession]]. Very strong effect and synergies with the rest of the deck. Cut due to not being on flavour, but should be highly considered due to strong combo potential with Elenda.

  • [[card:Bloodletter of Aclazotz]] - Essentially very similar to [[card: Wound Reflection]], but only works during your turn. Very strong effect and effectively halves the mana required to drain our opponents completely with [[card: Exsanguinate]]. Synergises with several cards in the deck, but not Elenda herself.

  • [[card:Defiant Bloodlord]] - Effect redundancy for [[card:Sanguine Bond]]. Has many synergies with lifegain cards within the deck, however has been cut due to the high CMC compared to similar cards.

  • [[card:Falkenrath Noble]] / [[card:Vindictive Vampire]] / [[card:Wispdrinker Vampire]] / [[card:Vein Ripper]] / [[card:Zulaport Cutthroat]] / [[card:Syr Konrad, the Grim]] / [[card:Bastion of Remembrance]] / [[card:Elas il-Kor, Sadistic Pilgrim]] / [[card:The Meathook Massacre]] / [[card: Funeral Room]] - Effect redundancy for [[card:Blood Artist]]. Has many synergies with death triggers within the deck, and are finishers for any infinite loop, however there are only so many effect redundancies which are required.

  • [[card:Hatred]] - Combo tech for Elenda, can knock out an opponent with commander damage, then with a sacrifice outlet, generate a lot of vampire tokens.

  • [[card:Despark]] / [[card:Path to Exile]] / [[card:Swords to Plowshares]] - Very efficient removal, but exiling creatures will deny us of death triggers. [[card:Anguished Unmaking]] was kept due to flavor and broader utility.

  • [[card:Butcher of Malakir]] / [[card:Dictate of Erebos]] / [[card:Grave Pact]] - Synergizes well with other aristocrats and death trigger strategies, helps to keep our opponents boards clear while we move in for the kill.

  • [[card:Gray Merchant of Asphodel]] / [[card:Kokusho, the Evening Star]] / [[card:Torment of Hailfire]] - These are additional finishers that work very similarly to [[card:Exsanguinate]] or [[card:Malakir Bloodwitch]]. Also the creatures can utilize recursive loops with [[card:Nim Deathmantle]] for added synergy.

  • [[card:Karmic Guide]] + [[card:Reveillark]] - A mainstay EDH / commander combo. With a sacrifice outlet in play, we can infinitely resurrect Elenda, creating infinite vampire tokens and infinite death triggers (synergizing with [[card:Blood Artist]] or [[card:Cruel Celebrant]]).

  • [[card:Ogre Slumlord]] / [[card:Pitiless Plunderer]] / [[card:Requiem Angel]] - Common inclusions as token producers, if going heavy on the token producing strategy. Essentially allows for more sacrifice fodder to power up Elenda.

  • [[card:Luminhous Broodmoth]] - Recursion engine for Elenda, which allows her to combo off earlier with recursive loops with [[card:Nim Deathmantle]] and [[card:Ashnod's Altar]]. Beware that Broodmoth's ability does not bring back tokens without flying, Cut due to not being on flavour.

  • [[card:Ayli, Eternal Pilgrim]] - Life gain synergies, as well as being an additional sacrifice outlet will often result in the conditions of her second activated ability being met. As a reuseable [[card:Anguished Unmaking]], she is a strong card under these conditions.

===endpanel ===panel: Budget Options - "I foresook sustenance to bring myself closer." I've done my best to keep this deck affordable, and most of the key synergies in this deck are usually relatively obtainable. Keep in mind that prices do fluctuate over time. However, here I will list a number of suitable replacements for some of the more expensive cards listed (_priced approximately $5 or more_) as budget alternatives for the deck.

  • [[card:Ashnod's Altar]] + [[card:Nim Deathmantle]] - Another set of infinite combo pieces which synergise well with Elenda. The alternative combo is slightly cheaper, and has the benefit of being amenable to reanimation strategies.
    Alternatives: [[card:Karmic Guide]] + [[card:Reveillark]]

  • [[card:Phyrexian Altar]] - Phyxrexian Altar produces colored mana, which enables casting anything, including Elenda from the command zone when we go infinite. The alternative option is to utilise [[card:Ashnod's Altar]] for infinite colorless, then filter it through the following alternatives.
    Alternatives: [[card:Urn of Godfire]], [[card:Energy Refractor]], [[card:Prismite]], [[card:Stonework Packbeast]]

  • [[card:Austere Command]] - A flexible board wipe with a lot of utility. Can be replaced by any number of board wipes available in our colors.
    Alternatives: [[card:Slash the Ranks]], [[card:Merciless Eviction]], [[card:Martial Coup]], [[card:Kaya's Wrath]], [[card:Fumigate]]

  • [[card:Smothering Tithe]] - Mana ramp that will give us accrued value over time. It also has synergies with the rest of the deck, either through treasure tokens or death triggers.
    Alternatives: [[card:Pitiless Plunderer]], [[card:Sifter of Skulls]]

  • [[card:Bloodline Keeper]] / [[card:Kalitas, Traitor of Ghet]] - Token producers who are flavorful by being vampires. Can be replaced by other token producers to fulfil the same role.
    Alternatives: [[card:Legion's Landing]], [[card:Ogre Slumlord]], [[card:Requiem Angel]]

  • [[card:Exquisite Blood]] / [[card:Bloodthirsty Conqueror]] - Forms one half of the [[card:Exquisite Blood]] + [[card:Sanguine Bond]] combo. There is no similar effect on another card. Unfortunately effects that fulfill a similar role are also more expensive. The best budget substitutes would be ones that synergize well with the [[card:Sanguine Bond]] effect.
    Alternatives: [[card:Revival / Revenge]], [[card:Gray Merchant of Asphodel]], [[card:Wound Reflection]], [[card:Archfiend of Despair]]

  • [[card:Exsanguinate]] - The ability to mass drain your opponents causes a massive life swing, which can be vital to ending the game in your favor. This card also acts as a potential mana sink to win the game when you've obtained infinite mana.
    Alternatives: [[card:Revival / Revenge]], [[card:Gray Merchant of Asphodel]], [[card:Scholar of Athreos]], [[card:Profane Command]]

  • [[card:Necropotence]] / [[card:Skullclamp]] / [[card:Twilight Prophet]] - Repeatable card draw is incredibly useful for digging out your win conditions. While these are the most efficient, there are multiple alternatives available.
    Alternatives: [[card:Erebos, God of the Dead]], [[card:Arguel's Blood Fast]], [[card:Vampiric Rites]]

  • [[card:Sorin Markov]] - Sorin's ability to set an opponent's life total is pretty infamous in commander. Changing life totals also counts as gaining or losing life, and will trigger abilities based off of this. Interestingly, there are a number of suitable alternatives that are also rather flavorful.
    Alternatives: [[card:Axis of Mortality]], [[card:Repay in Kind]], [[card:Tree of Perdition]]

  • [[card:Vampiric Tutor]] - The most expensive card in the deck is a tutor mainly included for flavor, and can be foregone completely. Most tutors tend to run on the expensive side, so alternatives depend on budget. [[card:Forerunner of the Legion]] already exists in the deck as a partial tutor too.
    Alternatives: [[card:Diabolic Intent]], [[card:Diabolic Tutor]], [[card:Razaketh's Rite]]

===endpanel ===panel: Weaknesses - "The horrors that we wrought." The deck faces certain limitations due to the balance of the color pie, as well as the mechanics of Elenda herself. However, if faced with certain threats more commonly in your meta, it is worth consideration to have on hand certain substitutions in your sideboard.

  • Storm / Combo - The deck has no way to stop combos that are reliant on instants or sorceries. Your best bet is to remove key pieces or mana enablers before they can storm off. However, while noone expects counters from [[symbol:W]] or [[symbol:B]], you actually have options such as [[card:Lapse of Certainty]] or [[card:Mana Tithe]] as a surprise counter. Otherwise, all hope rests on another [[symbol:U]] player.

  • Voltron - The suite of board wipes we run help to protect Elenda, but they will also help protect your opponents' voltron commanders as well. Either run [[card:Arcane Lighthouse]] and [[card:Detection Tower]], then use your targeted removal, or replace your board wipes with less conditional ones, such as [[card:Damnation]] and [[card:Wrath of God]].

  • Reanimator / Aristocrats - Our deck generally likes to see our opponents creatures hit their graveyards, as it helps power our synergies, so it doesn't help when it is their strategy too. Cards like [[card:Relic of Progenitus]], [[card:Crook of Condemnation]], [[card:Graveyard Shovel]], and [[card:Scrabbling Claws]] help us target specific threats in an opponent's graveyard, while also being alternative ways to bring Elenda back from our graveyard to the command zone.

  • Targeted Removal - Elenda typically doesn't need to stay on the battlefield for long to accumulate counters if you play strategically. As such, we forego the staple suite of [[card:Lightning Greaves]] and [[card:Swiftfoot Boots]] for protection. In addition, added synergies make cards such as [[card:Spirit Bonds]] or [[card:Together Forever]] useful.

  • Graveyard Hate - Elenda is heavily reliant on death triggers, as is much of the deck. While the deck has answers to cards like [[card:Rest In Peace]], consider running heavier enchantment removal, including [[card:Return to Dust]] and [[card:Utter End]] in addition.

===endpanel ===panel: Oathbreaker - "The most devoted of the devoted." Oathbreaker is a 60 card singleton format, which is a variant on Commander / EDH. Players start with 20 life, which results in faster games. Rather than a commander, you have a planeswalker in your command zone who is your "Oathbreaker", along with an instant or sorcery card which is your "Signature Spell" that can be cast anytime your Oathbreaker is on the field. Both the Oathbreaker and Signature Spell each accumulate an additional [[symbol:2]] mana tax for each time they've been previously cast.

The mana base of this deck has been designed so that by removing all but one of each basic [[card:Swamp]] and [[card:Plains]], you are left with 24 lands in the deck. Along with 27 more cuts, this commander deck can be modified into an Oathbreaker deck. I would suggest cutting the cards which are heavily reliant on Elendra, as well as the higher CMC cards. An example decklist would be as follows:

  • Oathbreaker - [[card:Sorin, Vengeful Bloodlord]]
  • Signature Spell - [[card:Anguished Unmaking]]
  • Alternate Signature Spell - [[card:Vampiric Tutor]]

  • Decklist - [[card:Blade of the Bloodchief]], [[card:Blood Artist]], [[card:Blood Tribute]], [[card:Cathar's Crusade]], [[card:Cordial Vampire]], [[card:Cruel Celebrant]], [[card:Drana and Linvala]], [[card:Debt to the Deathless]], [[card:Divine Reckoning]], [[card:Elenda, the Dusk Rose]], [[card:Exquisite Blood]], [[card:Exsanguinate]], [[card:Felisa Fang of Silverquill]], [[card:Fellwar Stone]], [[card:Go for the Throat]], [[card:Kalitas, Traitor of Ghet]], [[card:Malakir Bloodwitch]], [[card:Marauding Blight-Priest]], [[card:Monologue Tax]], [[card:Mind Stone]], [[card:Mortify]], [[card:Orzhov Signet]], [[card:Pawn of Ulamog]], [[card:Revel in Riches]], [[card:Sanctum Seeker]], [[card:Sanguine Bond]], [[card:Sign in Blood]], [[card:Smothering Tithe]], [[card:Talisman of Hierarchy]], [[card:Tragic Arrogance]], [[card:Welcoming Vampire]], [[card:Vindicate]], [[card:Yahenni, Undying Partisan]]

  • Lands - [[card:Bojuka Bog]], [[card:Brightclimb Pathway]], [[card:Castle Lochthwain]], [[card:Caves of Koilos]], [[card:Command Tower]], [[card:Concealed Courtyard]], [[card:Emergence Zone]], [[card:Fabled Passage]], [[card:Fetid Heath]], [[card:Forsaken Sanctuary]], [[card:Godless Shrine]], [[card:Hall of Heliod's Generosity]], [[card:High Market]], [[card:Isolated Chapel]], [[card:Orzhov Basilica]], [[card:Path of Ancestry]], [[card:Phyrexian Tower]], [[card:Plains]], [[card:Swamp]], [[card:Tainted Field]], [[card:Thespian's Stage]], [[card:Vault of Champions]], [[card:Vault of the Archangel]], [[card:Westvale Abbey]]
    • ===endpanel ===endaccordion ===endpanel ===endaccordion
    ===accordion ===panel: Updates
    ===accordion ===panel: Update 2.08 - Tarkir: Dragonstorm — Apr. 11, 2025 Primer - Small update to the primer to discuss a couple of flexible options in the new set.

    Card Evaluation
  • [[card: Elspeth, Storm Slayer]] - The new Elspeth card is a token doubler, token generator, removal, +1/+1 counter generator, and potential combat finisher all rolled into one. Although costing 1 mana more, I would say this is a preferable card over [[card: Mondrak, Glory Dominus]] due to its flexibility.
  • [[card: Qarsi Revenant]] - An upgraded [[card: Vampire Nighthawk]] which is a great card in isolation, but doesn't synergise well with our game plan.
  • [[card: Barrensteppe Siege]] - The Abzan mode of this card is probably going to be the most relevant, placing a +1/+1 counter on each creature we control. However, timing wise it occurs on our end step, which means that this can be quite slow to get going, and won't be of benefit on the turn you play it.
  • [[card: Will of the Mardu]] - The token generation modality is going to be the most relevant here. If a player has three or more creatures, casting this spell will generally be efficient for token generation for its cost. Add in the flexibility as an instant, for saving yourself against a wide attack, being able to deal direct damage to a creature to remove it, and you have a strong flexible card that synergises well with the deck.
  • [[card: Will of the Abzan]] - Generally will be three for one removal, but also can be used to return a creature card from the graveyard. Another flexible spell, but held back by sorcery speed.
    • ===endpanel ===panel: Update 2.08 - Aetherdrift — Feb. 14, 2025 Primer - Small update to the primer to discuss a couple of flexible options in the new set.

    Commander Brackets - With the unveiling of the Commander Bracket system, this deck falls under Bracket 3: Upgraded. The two Game Changer cards which push it over the line from Bracket 2 are:
  • [[card: Smothering Tithe]] - Game Changer card, due to providing an overwhelming mana advantage which can snowball games.
  • [[card: Vampiric Tutor]] - Game Changer card, due to being a best in class tutor.

    Decklist Changes
  • [[card: Path of Ancestry]] > [[card: Bleachbone Verge]] - [[card: Path of Ancestry]] enters tapped, while the scry ability is helpful in the late game for card filtering, it's slower early.

    • Card Evaluation
  • [[card: Bleachbone Verge]] - Part of a new cycle of verge lands, this is essentially an upgrade over [[card: Tainted Field]] and [[card: Isolated Chapel]].
  • [[card: The Aetherspark]] - Has the potential to slowly accumulate +1/+1 counters on Elenda. If attached to Elenda while she has a lot of counters built up, can be used to generate a lot of mana.
    • ===endpanel ===panel: Update 2.07 - Foundations — Nov. 15, 2024 Primer - Small update to the primer to discuss a couple of flexible options in the new set.

    Decklist Changes
  • [[card: Redemption Choir]] > [[card: Bloodthirsty Conqueror]] - Redemption Choir has some tricky hoops to jump through which can make it inconsistent. Bloodthirsty Conqueror is an amazing card. It is an extra copy of the [[card: Exquisite Blood]] effect, but due to being a creature, is much easier to bring back from the graveyard, and potentially tutor from our deck. This makes it much easier to enable combos, fundamentally strengthening the deck power significantly.

    • Card Evaluation
  • [[card: Bloodthirsty Conqueror]] - As mentioned above, this is functionally [[card: Exquisite Blood]] on a creature, making it a very powerful combo card.
  • [[card: Elenda, Saint of Dusk]] - The new Elenda serves as a potential lifegain commander beatstick, and while thematically would fit in the deck, doesn't otherwise serve any purpose.
  • [[card: Fumulus, the Infestation]] - Has the potential to generate additional tokens to pump up Elenda if we are sacrificing things to altars, but not the best synergy for its cost.
  • [[card: High-Society Hunter]] - Potential card draw, works extremely well during board wipes, as it will have multiple triggers. Worthwhile consideration for card draw options if set up correctly.
  • [[card: Nazar, the Velvet Fang]] - Another potential card draw engine that works well off of lifegain, however, he does need to attack, which does slow down the potential payoff.
  • [[card: Vampire Gourmand]] - Another potential card draw engine, however, you need other creatures or tokens on the field to sacrifice to get going.
  • [[card: Vampire Soulcaller]] - A slightly more expensive [[card: Gravedigger]] effect, but on theme.
  • [[card: Vengeful Bloodwitch]] - A strong consideration as a sidegrade to [[card: Cruel Celebrant]], doesn't trigger off of planeswalkers, and only targets one opponent at a time. In the setting of going infinite via combo, the more relaxed casting cost is better overall.
    • ===endpanel ===panel: Update 2.06 - Duskmourn: House of Horror — Sep. 27, 2024 Primer - Small update to the primer to discuss a couple of flexible options in the new set.

    Decklist Changes
    No deck changes made this update.

    Card Evaluation
  • [[card: Enduring Tenacity]] - Redundancy effect for [[card: Sanguine Bond]], for 1 mana less, and more difficult to permanently remove.
  • [[card: Into the Pit]] - Sacrifice outlet which allows you to cast off the top of your library. However, depending on the card, limitations on when you can use it as an outlet, thus less flexibility compared to the altars.
  • [[card: Funeral Room]] - Another potential finisher option, with the flexibility of returning creatures to the battlefield.
  • [[card: Withering Torment]] - Instant speed removal for creatures and enchantments in mono-black. However in our colors, we have better options in general.
  • [[card: Lunar Convocation]] - Potential sidegrade for other enchantment draw options, such as [[card: Greed]].
  • [[card: Three Tree City]] - Typal commander staple land that will fit in most typal decks.
    • ===endpanel ===panel: Update 2.05 - Outlaws of Thunder Junction — Apr. 19, 2024 Primer - Small update to the primer to discuss a couple of vampire options with the new set.

    Decklist Changes
    No deck changes made this update.

    Card Evaluation
  • [[card: Baron Bertram Graywater]] - Token producer, but the effect is only once per turn. More efficient if running other token producers for Treasure/Blood/Food/Clues.
  • [[card: Blood Hustler]] - A potential budget finisher after generating infinite mana, can be dumped into its ability.
  • [[card: Marshland Bloodcaster]] - Interesting effect that allows us to pay life rather than mana to cast one spell per turn. However, the ability itself costs 2 mana, so is limited to more expensive spells in the deck. The flavor text is a big win though.
  • [[card: Ruthless Lawbringer]] - [[card: Vindicate]] on a creature. Potentially reusable with reanimation.
  • [[card: Veinwitch Coven]] - Brings creature cards from the graveyard back to our hand. Useful for us to refill following a board wipe.
  • [[card: Final Showdown]] - If paying for the spree costs, can be a board wipe while saving Elenda, but is expensive for the effect.
  • [[card: Insatiable Avarice]] - Flexible card draw and tutor.
  • [[card: One Last Job]] - Allows us to potentially re-use lost Aura or Equipment such as [[card: Gift of Immortality]] if they are lost to the graveyard.
  • [[card: Rush of Dread]] - Useful removal to help power Elenda, and also has a life loss effect which can potentially combo with other cards.
  • [[card: Lavaspur Boots]] - Budget [[card: Lightning Greaves]].
  • [[card: Requisition Raid]] - Artifact / enchantment removal which has flexibility for adding +1/+1 counters if we need them.
    • ===endpanel ===panel: Update 2.04 - Murders at Karlov Manor — Feb. 09, 2024 Primer - Small update to the primer to discuss a couple of vampire options with the new set.

    Decklist Changes
    No deck changes made this update.

    Card Evaluation
  • [[card: Vein Ripper]] - Potential combo ender with its drain effect. However, mana cost is too high for its effect. While the protection from ward is helpful, the other parts of the combo would still remain vulnerable.
  • [[card: Wispdrinker Vampire]] - Another combo finisher, which synergises with Elenda's token creation. Also has the benefit of potentially giving our tokens deathtouch (also gives lifelink but the tokens already have it). Probably a direct upgrade over [[card: Vindictive Vampire]], if it's in your deck.
    • ===endpanel ===panel: Update 2.03 - Lost Caverns of Ixalan — Nov. 17, 2023 Primer - It's been over 5 years since the original Ixalan set. With the release of the *Blood Rites* commander preconstructed deck, there are a few new cards to evaluate.

    Decklist Changes
  • [[card: Pitiless Pontiff]] > [[card: Bartolomé del Presidio]] - Strict upgrade for a sacrifice outlet on a vampire. Bartolomé can sacrifice for free, can utilise treasure tokens if required, and generates +1/+1 counters which can synergise with our commander and cards like [[card: Animation Module]].
  • [[card: Kalitas, Traitor of Ghet]] > [[card: Charismatic Conqueror]] - Both create tokens, but Charismatic Conqueror is cheaper, and creates vampire tokens for more synergy. Kalitas is more useful as a sideboard for graveyard hate.
  • [[card: Cataclysmic Gearhulk]] > [[card: Redemption Choir]] - Redemption Choir is able to bring back key combo pieces that are lost to the graveyard, including our altars, which is more valuable than the board wipe.
  • [[card: Patron of the Vein]] > [[card: Carmen, Cruel Skymarcher]] - Like Redemption Choir, having means to bring back key combo pieces is useful. Patron relies heavily on removing enemy creatures to build counters on our board, more suited to decks with cards like [[card: Butcher of Malakir]]. Patron is another useful sideboard for graveyard hate.
  • [[card: Vault of the Archangel]] > [[card: Silent Clearing]] - Flexible mana is more useful over niche use for deathtouch. Most of our creatures will already have lifelink.

    • Card Evaluation
  • [[card: Amalia Benavides Aguirre]] - Potential board wipe, requires a different commander build to setup.
  • [[card: Bartolomé del Presidio]] - Sacrifice outlet, +1/+1 counter generator, low casting cost, very good synergy with out strategies.
  • [[card: Bloodletter of Aclazotz]] - Combos with [[card: Blood Tribute]] and [[card: Sorin Markov]].
  • [[card: Bringer of the Last Gift]] - [[card: Living Death]] on a vampire/demon body. Can revive our board in a pinch, but too expensive for general play.
  • [[card: Preacher of the Schism]] - A cross between [[card: Mavren Fein, Dusk Apostle]] and [[card: Phyrexian Arena]] type effect, but not entirely effective as either.
  • [[card: Queen's Bay Paladin]] - A useful reusable revival effect for vampires, however the finality effect reduces the ability to loop/combo.
  • [[card: Sanguine Evangelist]] - Single use token producer. Efficient for cost, but not useful otherwise.
  • [[card: Tarrian's Soulcleaver]] - Redundancy effect for [[card: Blade of the Bloodchief]]. Note that it does have a higher equip cost, and doesn't do additional value for vampires, but does activate with artifact tokens including treasures.
  • [[card: Vanguard of the Rose]] - Sacrifice outlet with similar effect to [[card: Pitiless Pontiff]].
  • [[card: Vito, Fanatic of Aclazotz]] - Triggered effect only occurs three times per turn, and generally build is in a different direction, towards vampire/demon hybrids.
  • [[card: Carmen, Cruel Skymarcher]] - Interesting alternative commander, combos well with edict effects such as [[card: Fleshbag Marauder]]. Has some synergy with Elenda, with +1/+1 counters, and can bring sacrificed vampires back.
  • [[card: Charismatic Conqueror]] - Gives opponents the choice between an [[card: Blind Obedience]] effect vs. giving us a token. Can often become an efficient token generator.
  • [[card: Elenda's Hierophant]] - Another +1/+1 counter and token support.
  • [[card: Dusk Legion Sergeant]] - Unfortunately doesn't combo well with Elenda (given she is a 1/1 creature), thus she doesn't benefit from *persist*. May be useful as an enabler to preserve the rest of the board.
  • [[card: March of the Canonized]] - Potential token generator if we meet the required devotion.
  • [[card: Master of Dark Rites]] - Sacrifice outlet which enables a [[card: Dark Ritual]] effect each turn. Effective ramp to bring Elenda out early.
  • [[card: Order of Sacred Dusk]] - Has the potential to grant Elenda exalted, which can power her up based on the number of tokens you have available. In and of itself, can be used as a potential finisher.
  • [[card: Promise of Aclazotz]] - Can convert vampire tokens into 4/3 vampire demon tokens. Slow effect at once per turn, and doesn't otherwise synergise with our planned strategy.
  • [[card: Redemption Choir]] - A cheaper [[card: Sun Titan]] effect, with a couple of hoops to get through. However, with another vampire and token out, it may be fairly easy to fulfil these requirements. Returning permanents to the battlefield also gives us a means to return important artifacts including our altars.
    • ===endpanel ===panel: Update 2.02 - March of the Machine — Apr. 21, 2023 Primer - I've updated the primer to discuss other strong cards that didn't make the list due to flavour, most notably [[card: Drivnod, Carnage Dominus]] and [[card: Mondrak, Glory Dominus]].

    Decklist Changes - It's been a couple of years since I've made updates to the deck, but trying to optimise further with cards from the latest sets.
  • [[card: Necropotence]] > [[card: Land Tax]] - [[symbol:b]][[symbol:b]][[symbol:b]] is a bit more intensive to setup, Land Tax drops earlier, both help to refill the hand, although Necropotence has better utility.
  • [[card: Austere Command]] > [[card: Cathars' Crusade]] - There are more than sufficient board wipes in the deck, and Cathar's Crusade is an additional win condition with +1/+1 counter synergies with other cards.
  • [[card: Dark Impostor]] > [[card: Drana and Linvala]] - Essentially performing the same utility in shutting down opponent abilities, to allow more time to setup our own combos. However, Dark Impostor requires much heavier investment to activate, and we are usually looking to combo off at that stage.
  • [[card: Bloodline Keeper]] > [[card: Felisa, Fang of Silverquill]] - Bloodline Keeper gives consistent token production, however, Felisa acts as another combo enabler for Elenda.
  • [[card: Mavren Fein, Dusk Apostle]] > [[card: Monologue Tax]] - Mavren Fein was mainly kept for flavour, but had to be cut because 1 token per turn was inefficient. Monologue Tax enables ramp, and synergises with the Revel in Riches win-con.
  • [[card: Syphon Mind]] > [[card: Welcoming Vampire]] - Syphon Mind gives a hand refill, but draws hate because of the discard, and doesn't synergise with our other strategies. Welcoming Vampire brings utility with many creatures or tokens in the deck triggering card draw.
    • ===endpanel ===panel: Update 2.01 - Commander Legends — Nov. 20, 2020 Decklist Changes - Predominantly optimizing the mana base, more untapped mana is useful as we lower the mana curve to try and setup faster.
  • [[card:Evolving Wilds]] > [[card:Brightclimb Pathway]] - Optimizing mana base with untapped flexible mana.
  • [[card:Terramorphic Expanse]] > [[card:Vault of Champions]] - Optimizing mana base with untapped flexible mana.
  • [[card:Shambling Vent]] > [[card:Fabled Passage]] - Optimizing mana base with untapped flexible mana.
  • [[card:Reliquary Tower]] > [[card:Westvale Abbey]] - Alternate win condition added, with synergy for token strategies. Unlimited hand size was only useful with Necropotence in play.
  • [[card:Mind Stone]] > [[card:The Ozolith]] - Powerful addition from Ikoria, which allows us to keep any progress in +1/+1 counters, should Elenda be removed. Lowered mana curve allows us to sacrifice some mana ramp.
  • [[card:Cliffhaven Vampire]] > [[card:Marauding Blight-Priest]] - Lowers the mana curve, while effectively fulfilling the same role. The flying and bulkiness on Cliffhaven is negligible. ===endpanel ===panel: Update 2.00 - Core Set 2021 — July 3, 2020 Commander Rules Change - If a commander has an ability which triggers on it dying or going to exile, it will trigger before heading to the command zone. This new rules change makes Elenda that much more powerful, as we can reliably bring her back from the command zone, while still getting her death trigger payoff. As such, some of the niche recursion cards can be cut for more synergy.
  • [[card:Defiant Bloodlord]] > [[card:Vito, Thorn of the Dusk Rose]] - Replacing a similar effect with pushed, lower curve version.
  • [[card:Butcher of Malakir]] > [[card:Anointed Procession]] - Lowering curve, and enabling more combos.
  • [[card:Black Market]] > [[card:Phyrexian Altar]] - Death trigger changes now allow additional combos with Phyrexian Altar.
  • [[card:Memorial to Folly]] > [[card:Castle Locthwain]] - Graveyard recursion now less necessary than card draw.
  • [[card:Mistveil Plains]] > [[card:Hall of Heliod's Generosity]] - Graveyard recursion for enchantments over commander.
  • [[card:Mortuary Mire]] > [[card:Phyrexian Tower]] - Graveyard recursion now less necessary than sacrifice outlet.
    • ===endpanel ===panel: Update 1.01 - Thrones of Eldraine — Oct. 4, 2019
  • [[card: Gatekeeper of Malakir]] > [[card: Arcane Signet]] - Focus on efficient mana-ramping to bring Elenda out, rather than conditional removal.
    • ===endpanel ===endaccordion ===endpanel ===endaccordion
    Hope you found this primer useful/informative, thank you for your piety and support!

    "Your reverence is manifest, but your mission will require your full awareness"
